message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix J-002 in scripts/capture_transcripts.py
What: operator full display name redacted (from git user.name / ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES), whitespace-flexible + length-guarded, applied in _scrub.
Test(s): test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
commit 20771893171982c3d4f51b51b2738c15da22ad0b
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 02:35:46 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p7): 6 findings incl. 2 highs (J-001 fix-regression of CV-O-1) + J-002 PII
    
    Pass-7 applied the pass-6 dimensions (per-dimension coverage matrix + fix-regression audit).
    The fix-regression audit immediately caught a HIGH the prior pass introduced. 6/7 confirmed
    (J-007 MANIFEST rejected); all revert-discriminated.
    
    - J-001 (HIGH, fix-regression of #CV-O-1) the scrubber's strict UTF-8 decode FAILED OPEN: one
      invalid byte routed the whole transcript to verbatim copy, re-leaking secrets/PII. Now decodes
      errors="replace" (always scrubs) and classifies binary by a NUL byte, not UTF-8 validity.
    - J-002 (HIGH) the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (git user.name / ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES) was never
      redacted — distinct PII beside already-redacted emails (252 committed files). Added
      whitespace-flexible, length-guarded display-name redaction; pre-commit re-capture cleans the tree.
    - J-003 (med) the build backend (hatchling) was fetched live + un-hashed during `pip install -e .`
      (PEP-517 build isolation), an unscanned/non-reproducible build-time code-exec window. Added a
      hashed requirements-build.lock + `--no-build-isolation` install + a sync-check.
    - J-004 (med) TOCTOU in MlflowExperimentTracker._experiment_id: concurrent first-time start_run
      could lose the whole run; now re-fetches on a create race instead of propagating.
    - J-005 (med) the T3 transient-error tests asserted the base ReasoningError (which RateLimitError
      and PermanentReasoningError subclass), so a mis-classification passed; now assert the exact
      transient class.
    - J-006 (low) license gate scans the dependency set, not entire site-packages (dropped
      --with-system).
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 541 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

 .github/workflows/ci.yml                           |  17 +-
 requirements-build.lock                            |  26 ++
 scripts/capture_transcripts.py                     |  54 +++-
 src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py         |  14 +-
 tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py          |  18 +-
 tests/test_observability.py                        |  26 ++
 tests/test_scripts.py                              |  33 ++
 tests/test_supply_chain.py                         |  22 ++
 transcripts/MANIFEST.json                          | 350 ++++++++++++++++++++-

143	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "t.jsonl"

148	
149	def test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name(monkeypatch):
150	    # #J-002: the operator's full display name (from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES / git user.name) is redacted,
151	    # not just the email — distinct PII that leaked beside redacted emails.
152	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "Ada Lovelace")
153	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_name")
154	    out = cap._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
155	    assert "Ada Lovelace" not in out and "Ada   Lovelace" not in out  # flexible whitespace too
156	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # email still scrubbed
157	
158	
159	def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
160	    # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
161	    # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
162	    src = (_ROOT / "examples" / "minimal_study.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
163	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
164	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place

Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>

    fix(hardening-p7): 6 findings incl. 2 highs (J-001 fix-regression of CV-O-1) + J-002 PII
    
    Pass-7 applied the pass-6 dimensions (per-dimension coverage matrix + fix-regression audit).
    The fix-regression audit immediately caught a HIGH the prior pass introduced. 6/7 confirmed
    (J-007 MANIFEST rejected); all revert-discriminated.
    
    - J-001 (HIGH, fix-regression of #CV-O-1) the scrubber's strict UTF-8 decode FAILED OPEN: one
      invalid byte routed the whole transcript to verbatim copy, re-leaking secrets/PII. Now decodes
      errors="replace" (always scrubs) and classifies binary by a NUL byte, not UTF-8 validity.
    - J-002 (HIGH) the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (git user.name / ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES) was never
      redacted — distinct PII beside already-redacted emails (252 committed files). Added
      whitespace-flexible, length-guarded display-name redaction; pre-commit re-capture cleans the tree.
    - J-003 (med) the build backend (hatchling) was fetched live + un-hashed during `pip install -e .`
      (PEP-517 build isolation), an unscanned/non-reproducible build-time code-exec window. Added a
      hashed requirements-build.lock + `--no-build-isolation` install + a sync-check.
    - J-004 (med) TOCTOU in MlflowExperimentTracker._experiment_id: concurrent first-time start_run
      could lose the whole run; now re-fetches on a create race instead of propagating.
    - J-005 (med) the T3 transient-error tests asserted the base ReasoningError (which RateLimitError

diff --git a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
index 465013e..0c88f4d 100755
--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
@@ -73,7 +73,40 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
 
 
+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
+
+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
+    over-redacting common words.
+    """
+    names: list[str] = []
+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
+    try:
+        import subprocess
+
+        out = subprocess.run(
+            ["git", "config", "user.name"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5
+        )
+        if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
+            names.append(out.stdout.strip())
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
+        pass
+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
+    for n in names:
+        if len(n) < 4:
+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words
+        # match the name with flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]")
+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\b" + r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
+    return pats
+
+
 _USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
+_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
 
 
 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
@@ -88,6 +121,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
     # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
     if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
         text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
+    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
+    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
+    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
+        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
     return text
 
 
@@ -104,13 +141,18 @@ def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
         # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
         # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
         # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
-        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
-        try:
-            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
-        except UnicodeDecodeError:
-            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
+        #
+        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
+        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
+        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
+        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
+        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
+        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
+        # present) is copied verbatim.
+        if b"\x00" in raw:
+            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
         else:
-            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
+            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
         if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
             pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
         else:

content.text: The diff is clear. Let me verify the test discriminates by checking the module-level evaluation timing and running the test, then reverting.

content.content:
1	"""Guards for repo scripts + examples (pass 3: #F-051, #F-048).
2	
3	These exercise non-package files (scripts/, examples/) that the wheel never ships but that run
4	on every commit (transcript capture) or are copied by users (the example). Where a test targets
5	a file's *content* it reads THAT file and asserts both the corrected form and the absence of the
6	stale/insecure form (the artifact-vs-test fidelity rule).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import importlib.util
12	from pathlib import Path
13	
14	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
15	
16	
17	def _load(rel: str, name: str):
18	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, _ROOT / rel)
19	    assert spec and spec.loader
20	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
21	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
22	    return mod
23	
24	
25	def test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars():
26	    # #F-051: a bearer token containing base64 chars (+ / =) must be FULLY redacted, not split at
27	    # the first such char leaving a trailing fragment in the transcript.
28	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
29	    text = "Authorization: [REDACTED]"
30	    scrubbed = cap._scrub(text)
31	    assert "[REDACTED]" in scrubbed
32	    # no recognizable token fragment survives
33	    for frag in ("eyJhbGc", "payload/signature", "/signature", "iOiJIUzI1NiJ9"):
34	        assert frag not in scrubbed, f"token fragment {frag!r} leaked past redaction"
35	
36	
37	def test_license_gate_closes_the_report_file(tmp_path):
38	    # #F-050: the report must be opened via a context manager so the fd is closed deterministically.
39	    # Discriminating: intercept the module's open() and capture the file handle; after main()
40	    # returns, assert it is .closed. A bare json.load(open(...)) leaves the handle open (closed

content.text: Note: the test sets `ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES` via monkeypatch BEFORE `_load`, so the module-level `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()` picks it up at import time. The `_load` helper re-imports a fresh module each call with a unique name, so the env is read fresh. Good design. Let me run the test and the revert check.

Wait — `git checkout` updated 0 paths, meaning my cp restore already matched HEAD. But the diff --stat earlier showed 5+/3- which was stale (snapshot). The concern is real: **the test passed with the J-002 application loop removed.** Let me investigate why — likely the `_USERNAME_RE` (from J-001/earlier username redaction) already catches "Ada Lovelace", OR the test's email scrub masks it.

Let me reproduce the reverted state and inspect the actual output.

59	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
60	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
61	
62	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
63	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
64	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
65	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
66	    """
67	    try:
68	        name = Path.home().name
69	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
70	        return None
71	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
72	        return None
73	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
74	
75	
76	def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
77	    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
78	
79	    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
80	    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
81	    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
82	    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
83	    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
84	    over-redacting common words.
85	    """
86	    names: list[str] = []
87	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
88	    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
89	    try:
90	        import subprocess
91	
92	        out = subprocess.run(
93	            ["git", "config", "user.name"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5
94	        )
95	        if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
96	            names.append(out.stdout.strip())
97	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
98	        pass
99	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
100	    for n in names:
101	        if len(n) < 4:
102	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words
103	        # match the name with flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]")
104	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\b" + r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
105	    return pats
106	
107	
108	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
109	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
110	
111	
112	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
113	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
114	        if pat.groups == 2:
115	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
116	        elif pat.groups == 1:
117	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
118	        else:
119	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
120	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
121	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
122	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
123	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
124	    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
125	    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
126	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
127	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
128	    return text
129	
130	
131	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
132	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
133	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)

content.text: I see the issue. The username regex (`_USERNAME_RE`) is derived from `Path.home().name`. On this machine, the home dir is likely `[REDACTED]`. The test name "Ada Lovelace" should NOT be caught by that. Let me actually inspect why the reverted test passed — reproduce carefully with the test's monkeypatched env.

p="scripts/capture_transcripts.py"
s=open(p).read()
block='''    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
'''
assert block in s, "block not found"
open(p,"w").write(s.replace(block,""))
print("removed J-002 loop")
EOF
echo "=== verify removed ==="
grep -c "J-002) — distinct PII the username" scripts/capture_transcripts.py
echo "=== reproduce test manually ==="
ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES="Ada Lovelace" python3 - <<'EOF'
import importlib.util, pathlib
spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("cap_test","scripts/capture_transcripts.py")
m=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(m)
out=m._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
print("OUTPUT:", repr(out))
print("'Ada Lovelace' in out:", "Ada Lovelace" in out)
print("'Ada   Lovelace' in out:", "Ada   Lovelace" in out)
print("'[REDACTED]' in out:", "[REDACTED]" in out)
print("_DISPLAY_NAME_RES count:", len(m._DISPLAY_NAME_RES))
EOF

content.text:
Confirmed. Earlier false "pass" was a stale `.pyc`/module-cache artifact from running both reverted and original in the same interpreter session; the isolated re-run discriminates correctly.

Assessment of the fix:

**Appropriate:**
- `_display_name_patterns()` correctly derives names from `ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES` (comma-split, stripped) and `git config user.name` via subprocess with `timeout=5` and broad exception guard (capture must never break) — idiomatic and matches the existing `_username_pattern()` style.
- Length guard (`len(n) < 4` skip) prevents over-redacting short common words, mirroring the existing username rule.
- Whitespace-flexible regex `r"\b" + r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"` correctly matches "Ada   Lovelace" (multi-space) while `re.escape` on each token prevents regex injection. Word boundaries limit false positives.
- Applied in `_scrub` after the username/email rules, consistent with the documented ordering rationale.
- Module-level `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES` is computed once at import; the test's per-call `_load` re-imports so env changes are honored.

**Complete / discriminating:**
- `tests/test_scripts.py::test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name` passes on the fixed code and FAILS when the `_scrub` application loop is reverted (verified via isolated pytest run: `AssertionError: 'Ada Lovelace' is contained here`).
- Test covers all three claims: plain name redaction, whitespace-flexible form, and that email scrubbing still works.

One minor (non-blocking) note: the test only exercises the `ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES` path, not the `git config user.name` path, and does not assert against over-redaction (short-name guard). The core PII-redaction behavior is properly discriminated, so this is a coverage nicety, not a defect.

Files: `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py` (lines 76-128), `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_scripts.py` (lines 149-156).

input.reasoning:
Read the changed file (scripts/capture_transcripts.py lines 76-128) and test (tests/test_scripts.py lines 149-156), and ran revert-discrimination.

APPROPRIATE: _display_name_patterns() derives names from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES (comma-split/stripped) and `git config user.name` via subprocess with timeout=5 and a broad exception guard so capture never breaks — idiomatic, mirroring the existing _username_pattern(). Length-guarded (len<4 skip) to avoid over-redacting common words. Whitespace-flexible pattern r"\b"+r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split())+r"\b" correctly matches multi-space "Ada   Lovelace"; re.escape on each token prevents regex injection; \b boundaries limit false positives. Applied in _scrub after username/email rules per the documented ordering. Module-level _DISPLAY_NAME_RES computed once at import; the test's per-call _load re-imports so monkeypatched env is honored. No regression — email/username/secret scrubbing paths untouched.

COMPLETE/DISCRIMINATING: test passes on fixed code. When I removed ONLY the J-002 application loop in _scrub and re-ran in an ISOLATED pytest process, the test FAILED with `AssertionError: 'Ada Lovelace' is contained here: Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>...`. (A first revert run falsely passed due to a stale module/.pyc cache from running reverted+original in the same interpreter; the clean isolated re-run discriminates correctly.) Test covers plain redaction, whitespace-flexible form, and confirms email is still scrubbed.

Minor non-blocking note: test exercises only the ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES path (not git user.name) and does not assert the short-name over-redaction guard — coverage nicety, not a defect. File restored to HEAD; tree clean.

content.text: CONFIRMED. The J-002 fix is appropriate and the test discriminates (fails when reverted in an isolated process).
